verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is canonically a secreted serine protease with no intrinsic TM domain. However, recent systematic characterization (Shen et al., Clin Cancer Res 2025) confirmed its cell surface expression in prostate cancer cell lines and patient-derived tumors by FACS and multiplex immunofluorescent staining. Expression is highly prostate-restricted with little-to-no expression elsewhere. Multiple clinical-stage cell-surface-directed therapeutics (KLK2×CD3 bispecific pasritamig, 225Ac-radioconjugate, CAR-T) establish outer-leaflet accessibility. Surface anchoring mechanism is not fully characterized but wash-resistant display is evidenced by flow cytometry. Tissue restriction to prostate/prostate cancer drives contextual rather than yes.
